item.title: This Week on The Analog Antiquarian 摘要
item.description:
This article is part of a series exploring the historical and literary context of Shakespeare's Henry VI, Part 1. It delves into the political turmoil and events leading up to the play, including the death of Henry V and the ensuing power struggles in England and France. The author examines the play's portrayal of key figures like Joan of Arc and the Duke of Bedford, and discusses how Shakespeare adapted historical sources for dramatic effect. The piece also reflects on the play's themes of honor, nationalism, and the fragility of power.
标签: Shakespeare, Henry VI, history, literature, theater, drama, England, France, Joan of Arc, power

item.title: The European Commission Response to Siri AI and the DMA 摘要
item.description:
European Commission spokesperson Thomas Regnier stated that Apple's decision not to roll out 'Siri AI' in the EU is solely Apple's, as the Digital Markets Act (DMA) does not prohibit new features. Apple requested an 18-month exemption from interoperability obligations, which the EC rejected, emphasizing that EU rules are non-negotiable. The EC argues that allowing only Siri AI would close the market to other AI agents, violating the DMA's goal of ensuring consumer choice and developer competition. Regnier's statement was posted on LinkedIn rather than the EC's official press site.
标签: Apple, Siri AI, European Commission, DMA, Digital Markets Act, interoperability, EU regulation, antitrust, consumer choice, gatekeeper

item.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 1) 摘要
item.description:
OpenAI and Anthropic have filed for IPOs despite burning billions annually with no clear path to profitability. OpenAI needs $865 billion over four years to meet commitments, while Anthropic has taken on $35 billion in off-balance-sheet debt for compute infrastructure, with Broadcom backstopping most of it. Both companies are using the theoretical concept of recursive self-improvement (RSI) as a potential excuse to delay IPOs, which the author argues is a sign of desperation and a bubble indicator, as no viable business model for LLMs has been demonstrated.
标签: AI, OpenAI, Anthropic, IPO, finance, bubble, RSI, debt, business model, tech industry

item.title: The WWDC 2026 Keynote and State of the Union on YouTube 摘要
item.description:
Apple's Developer app allows downloading local copies of WWDC sessions and the State of the Union, but not the keynote. The author suggests using YouTube to obtain a local copy of the keynote. The full State of the Union video is over an hour long, but Apple also released a 4.5-minute recap that is recommended for those who haven't watched the full version.
标签: Apple, WWDC, keynote, State of the Union, YouTube, developer, video, recap

item.title: Quoting Andrew Singleton 摘要
item.description:
A satirical piece from McSweeney's humorously critiques AI economics through a fictional story. Jenny's crematorium receives a $20 billion investment from John's propane company for 5% equity. Jenny burns $10 billion and pays John $10 billion for propane to incinerate the money. John reports $10 billion revenue from AI investments and values Jenny's business at $100 billion. A Forbes reporter assigned to profile them enters a polyamorous relationship, producing a glowing but financially vague article. The story mocks circular transactions and inflated valuations in the AI industry.
标签: AI, economics, satire, investment, valuation, humor, finance, tech bubble

item.title: Pluralistic: Google's new remote attestation scheme is every bit as terrible as its old remote attestation scheme (12 Jun 2026) 摘要
item.description:
Google's new remote attestation scheme is criticized as equally harmful as its previous attempts, such as Web Environment Integrity (WEI). The article argues that remote attestation forces users to disclose device configurations to servers, enabling servers to block ad-blockers and tracker-blockers. Google, a thrice-convicted monopolist, uses this to further lock down Android, undermining open alternatives like GrapheneOS. The author contends that this technical barrier complements Google's illegal commercial tactics to suppress competition and user privacy, ultimately serving authoritarian surveillance.
标签: Google, remote attestation, Android, privacy, antitrust, surveillance, open source, monopoly, web standards, technofeudalism
